video_title: How Robinhood Perps Use Lighter to Make a New DeFi Mullet
gist: Lighter founder/CEO Vlad Novakovski explains how Lighter's perpetuals engine now powers perps inside Robinhood Wallet (settled in USDG, revenue split 50/50 with Lighter's share funding token buybacks), and lays out Lighter's collateral roadmap, risk models, CFTC licensing path for decentralized perps, and the thesis that onchain perp trading is structurally taking share from centralized venues.

insights.claim: Lighter's perpetuals engine powers perps directly inside Robinhood Wallet under a 50/50 revenue share, where Lighter's 50% (like 100% of Lighter's own app revenue) is immediately accrued to token holders via buybacks.
insights.speaker: Vlad Novakovski, founder/CEO of Lighter
insights.so_what: Direct token-holder value accrual mechanism means Robinhood-driven retail perp volume flows into LIT buybacks, tying token price to distribution wins.
insights.quote: So the revenue is shared 50/50 and the 50% that goes to us is kind of immediately acrewed to token holders for buybacks as is 100% of the revenue from for lighter.

insights.claim: The Robinhood perps product settles in the USDG stablecoin and skews toward a different market mix than Lighter's own app, with early demand concentrated in tokenized stocks and meme tokens rather than crypto majors.
insights.speaker: Vlad Novakovski, founder/CEO of Lighter
insights.so_what: Signals where retail perp demand is going (equities and memes) and that USDG adoption gets a distribution boost from Robinhood.

insights.claim: Robinhood was an equity investor in Lighter's round in November 2025 and the perps partnership is framed as a first step toward a deepening relationship, rooted in Vlad Novakovski's decade-plus ties as an early Robinhood adviser.
insights.speaker: Vlad Novakovski, founder/CEO of Lighter
insights.so_what: Investor-plus-partner alignment lowers execution risk and suggests further product integrations (US access, more collateral) are likely.

insights.claim: Because Robinhood chain and Lighter both settle on Ethereum, market makers can move capital between the two 'Lighter domains' quickly and hedge across them, making the setup far closer to consolidated liquidity than the full fragmentation of running separate venues on Ethereum vs Solana.
insights.speaker: Vlad Novakovski, founder/CEO of Lighter
insights.so_what: Shared-settlement-layer design is Lighter's edge for scaling multiple front ends without splintering depth; a template rivals must match.

insights.claim: USDG is less liquid than USDC or USDT, so market makers may quote it less aggressively, but the offsetting draw of Robinhood's retail order flow (which market makers most want) leads Lighter to expect net-tighter quoting despite the collateral friction.
insights.speaker: Vlad Novakovski, founder/CEO of Lighter
insights.so_what: Retail flow quality can outweigh a weaker quote asset; watch realized spreads on Robinhood perps to test whether the trade-off holds.

insights.claim: Lighter's roadmap is to accept any major stablecoin, ETH, BTC, and tokenized stocks as collateral, with a liquidation model that applies higher fees and haircuts to riskier collateral rather than a flat rate.
insights.speaker: Vlad Novakovski, founder/CEO of Lighter
insights.so_what: Broadening eligible collateral widens the addressable trader base and is a differentiator versus stablecoin-only perp venues.

insights.claim: On Lighter the liquidation fee is roughly 1% when collateral is a stablecoin, but rises to about 2% to 2.5% for riskier collateral such as tokenized stocks or Solana to price in that collateral's volatility.
insights.speaker: Vlad Novakovski, founder/CEO of Lighter
insights.so_what: Concrete risk-pricing tiers show how DEX perp economics scale with collateral risk; a benchmark for comparing venue safety and cost.
insights.quote: If if if your collateral is is stable coin now if you're trading your collateral isation fee may not be 1% it could be higher

insights.claim: Tokenized-stock collateral was deliberately not enabled in the first week of the Robinhood chain rollout and is expected to come in the following weeks, since the underlying equities don't trade 24/7 and could dislocate from the tokenized price.
insights.speaker: Vlad Novakovski, founder/CEO of Lighter
insights.so_what: Flags a near-term product catalyst plus a specific risk (weekend/overnight dislocation) to monitor once equity collateral goes live.

insights.claim: Lighter claims it had the best performance in the October 10th market event with the least amount of auto-deleveraging and liquidations, which it cites as validation of its risk models.
insights.speaker: Vlad Novakovski, founder/CEO of Lighter
insights.so_what: A stress-test track record is a competitive selling point for market makers and large traders choosing a perp venue.
insights.quote: as we know in October 10th, we've had the best performance in terms of the least amount of ADLs and liquidations

insights.claim: Onchain perps were about 1% of the overall perp market when Lighter started building and are now roughly 10-20%, which Vlad Novakovski cites as proof the onchain-over-centralized thesis is playing out.
insights.speaker: Vlad Novakovski, founder/CEO of Lighter
insights.so_what: Quantifies a durable secular share shift toward onchain derivatives, the core bull case for DEX perp tokens.
insights.quote: when we started building lighter like onchain perks were 1% of the overall market. I think now it's between depending on how you look at it between 10 and 20%

insights.claim: Vlad Novakovski argues even centralized products will increasingly route through DeFi rails, citing the Telegram wallet integration where the product is centralized but chooses Lighter's decentralized rails underneath because onchain execution is faster, cheaper, more verifiable and more secure.
insights.speaker: Vlad Novakovski, founder/CEO of Lighter
insights.so_what: The 'DeFi mullet' pattern (centralized front, DeFi back) expands Lighter's TAM beyond crypto-native users to any consumer app.
insights.quote: their their product is actually centralized but they would still rather use defy rails underneath meaning lighter than uh than centralized rails

insights.claim: Perp volumes on crypto-native assets have stalled over the past six months while RWA perp volumes hit all-time highs, which Novakovski attributes to BTC and ETH being down on the year and RWA activity being driven by AI and geopolitics, and he expects a reversion toward last year's mix by Q3-Q4.
insights.speaker: Vlad Novakovski, founder/CEO of Lighter
insights.so_what: Frames current crypto-native perp weakness as cyclical, not structural, and gives a timeline (Q3-Q4) to watch for mean reversion.

insights.claim: Lighter's Telegram wallet integration ranks at the top of its partner-attribution revenue, and by volume only one builder code on Hyperliquid has done more, which would put the Telegram integration at number two on that comparison list.
insights.speaker: Vlad Novakovski, founder/CEO of Lighter
insights.so_what: Demonstrates Lighter can win large distribution deals and benchmarks its traction directly against Hyperliquid's builder-code ecosystem.

themes.theme: US regulatory pathway for DeFi perps

insights.claim: Lighter is working with the CFTC on a decentralized-perp license that would cover both Lighter's own front end and brokers like Robinhood plugging into it; no one holds such a license yet, and Novakovski expects approval in a relatively short time frame, unlocking US access.
insights.speaker: Vlad Novakovski, founder/CEO of Lighter
insights.so_what: A first-of-its-kind CFTC decentralized-perp license would open the large US retail market and give Lighter a regulatory moat.

insights.claim: Vlad Novakovski was appointed to the CFTC's innovation advisory committee and Lighter's LIT token is issued from a Delaware C Corp, positioning the protocol on a compliant onshore regulatory path alongside partners including Robinhood's founder.
insights.speaker: host / Vlad Novakovski, founder/CEO of Lighter
insights.so_what: Regulatory access and a US-corporate token structure could become a template for other DEX tokens seeking an onshore path.

insights.claim: Novakovski frames a regulatory division of labor where the CFTC handles leverage, risk and fair order matching for onchain perps while tokenization questions (whether tokens are securities, onchain KYC) sit with the SEC.
insights.speaker: Vlad Novakovski, founder/CEO of Lighter
insights.so_what: Clarifies which agency governs which part of the DeFi-perp/tokenized-stock stack, useful for handicapping regulatory timelines.
